missingness.unknown: applicable but not observed; prevents a comparable total score
missingness.na: genuinely inapplicable under an item policy; requires a reason and is excluded
missingness.missing: treated as unknown, never as partial or fail

CORE-EEAT.construct: content-quality controls for one declared content artifact
CORE-EEAT.unit_of_analysis: one content artifact at one observation date

O03.condition: the artifact contains comparable structured data

O05.condition: the artifact is an indexable web page eligible for structured data

O10.condition: multimedia is part of the declared artifact

E01.condition: original data is part of the content promise

E02.condition: the content claims a novel framework

E03.condition: primary research is part of the content promise

E04.condition: the content takes a contrarian position

E05.condition: original visuals are needed to support the artifact

Exp01.condition: first-person experience is claimed or required by the profile

Exp02.condition: sensory observation is material to the subject

A07.condition: knowledge-graph presence is material to the audit objective

T04.condition: a material connection, paid placement, or affiliate relationship exists

T08.condition: the artifact makes YMYL or other material-risk claims

CITE.construct: domain citation-trust signals relative to a declared peer cohort
CITE.unit_of_analysis: one domain and market at one observation date
CITE.benchmark_mode: peer-relative; absolute thresholds are diagnostic starting points only

C05.condition: a locked AI-answer query panel is declared

C06.condition: AI citations were observed on the locked panel

C07.condition: multiple AI engines are in scope

I06.benchmark: peer-relative by entity stage; domain age alone cannot fail trust

T06.benchmark: privacy-protected WHOIS is neutral absent contradictory ownership evidence

T09.condition: verified manual-action or deindex evidence; lack of private console access is unknown

E09.condition: multi-region reach is part of the domain objective

C3.construct: three separately observed influencer scopes: creator, content, and campaign
C3.unit_of_analysis: one qualified scope at one observation time; forecast and actual campaign reads are never merged

ACE.A1.definition: audience composition is measured and stable; brand fit is excluded

ACE.A3.definition: typical reach reliability across a stated window; campaign tier fit is excluded

ACE.C4.definition: commercial saturation and disclosed category history; a specific brand conflict is scored in ROI.O1

ACE.E3.definition: repeat audience action signals with source/date; campaign conversion is scored in ROI.I2

ROAS.construct: incremental paid-media contribution and operating quality under declared business constraints
ROAS.unit_of_analysis: one account/campaign portfolio, currency, attribution window, and observation period

item_definitions.R1: conversion instrumentation verified against an own-data truth set
item_definitions.R2: cross-platform attribution deduplicated and windows/currency normalized
item_definitions.R3: incremental contribution or profit measured against the declared target/control
item_definitions.R4: CAC/CPA and payback satisfy the declared business constraint
item_definitions.R5: marginal return is read after conversion lag with uncertainty stated
item_definitions.O1: claims and required disclosures are substantiated
item_definitions.O2: platform policy and restricted-category requirements are satisfied
item_definitions.O3: offer economics, eligibility, terms, and availability are explicit
item_definitions.O5: creative hook, format, accessibility, and fatigue state fit the placement
item_definitions.A1: brand and placement safety verified from the placement evidence
item_definitions.A3: negative keywords, exclusions, and suppression controls are maintained
item_definitions.A4: campaign/account structure supports the declared objective without avoidable overlap
item_definitions.A5: reach, overlap, and audience saturation are measured
item_definitions.S1: budget pacing stays within the declared plan and constraints
item_definitions.S2: bid strategy and learning-state changes are governed
item_definitions.S3: marginal CPC/CPM/CTR/CVR efficiency is compared on a normalized window
item_definitions.S4: frequency and creative decay are separated from audience saturation
item_definitions.S5: paid/organic and cross-campaign cannibalization are assessed

SEND.construct: email program integrity, engagement, lifecycle fit, and declared business outcome
SEND.unit_of_analysis: one sending program/profile and normalized observation window

item_definitions.S1: SPF/DKIM/DMARC alignment verified from DNS and aggregate evidence
item_definitions.S2: consent/lawful basis and acquisition provenance are on file
item_definitions.S3: inbox placement is measured on a declared provider/seed panel
item_definitions.S4: hard-bounce and complaint rates are normalized by cohort/window
item_definitions.S5: suppression, hygiene, and sunset controls are active
item_definitions.E1: click or downstream action rate is the primary engagement signal
item_definitions.E2: open/CTOR is used only with MPP segmentation and an explicit proxy caveat
item_definitions.E3: subject, preheader, and body promise match
item_definitions.E4: send timing and frequency fit preference and capacity
item_definitions.E5: engagement decay and reactivation/sunset logic are measured
item_definitions.N1: one-click opt-out works and live suppression tombstones are honored
item_definitions.N2: entry, confirmation, and welcome/first-touch logic fit the program
item_definitions.N3: applicable lifecycle journeys exist for the declared program type
item_definitions.N4: segmentation and progression logic use relevant evidence
item_definitions.N5: preference and frequency controls are available where applicable
item_definitions.D1: claims, disclosures, and offer terms match the claims ledger
item_definitions.D2: the declared outcome truth set is measured: revenue, pipeline, subscription, sponsorship, or another named outcome
item_definitions.D3: offer and CTA are clear for the declared program
item_definitions.D5: outcome attribution is reconciled outside provider self-reporting

E2.condition: opens or CTOR are used in the assessment

N3.condition: only journeys applicable to the declared program type are scored

N5.condition: the program offers recurring sends or configurable frequency

D2.benchmark: truth set follows the program: ecommerce, CRM pipeline, subscription, sponsorship, or declared equivalent

RAMP.construct: three non-interchangeable launch reads: preflight readiness, execution quality, and observed outcomes
RAMP.unit_of_analysis: one launch at one declared lifecycle read

R1.definition: declared lifecycle stage contradicts verifiable access/eligibility; a public pricing page is not universally required

P1.definition: required launch surfaces have verified measurement, using N/A for genuinely non-participating surfaces

ECHO.construct: separate social asset compliance and program operating maturity reads
ECHO.unit_of_analysis: one asset gate or one channel portfolio/window, never both in one score

C1.condition: the asset makes a product or offer claim

C2.condition: a material connection or realistic synthetic media exists

H2.condition: third-party UGC is republished outside a native share

O1.asset_gate_note: an observed asset with no performance rate or metric claim may pass this control; do not infer from missing asset access

ECHO.outcomes: reported as measured metrics with controls; no rubric score until outcome calibration exists

TALE.construct: separate narrative truth, system coherence, and measured effectiveness reads
TALE.unit_of_analysis: one canon/surface set or one message experiment at one observation date

T1.definition: material differentiation is false, contradictory, or unsubstantiated; a literal onlyness claim is not required

A2.condition: a message-house pattern is chosen; pillar count is user-justified rather than universally fixed

A4.condition: a change-narrative arc is appropriate to the strategy
